codified: written down as a formal rule
juxtaposition: placing side by side for comparison
extrapolate: use known facts to predict the unknown
metalanguage: vocabulary used to talk about language itself
propositions: statements that can be judged true or false
proposition: a statement that can be judged true or false
decomposition: breaking a problem down into smaller, more manageable parts
counter-examples: examples that prove a claim wrong
counter-example: an example that proves a claim wrong
justification: reasons or evidence given to support a claim
